action: Within Visual Studio Code, click on 'File' menu at the top, then select 'Open Folder…'. When file picker GUI appears, navigate to 'Documents' directory and select 'CalculatorApp' folder. Finally, click 'Select Folder' or 'OK' to open it.
fallback_action: Perform a Google search using Internet Explorer

recipe.steps: is the first action. The task is to open a folder in Visual Studio Code. The instructions are to click on the 'File' menu at the top, then select 'Open Folder…'. I can see the 'File' menu (Box ID 40) at the top of the Visual Studio Code window. The first step is to click on the 'File' menu.

previous action: I was instructed to click on the 'File' menu in Visual Studio Code. The current screen shows the File menu dropdown is open, with options such as 'New Text File', 'New File', 'New Window', 'Open Folder', etc. This confirms the previous action succeeded. The next step is to select 'Open Folder…' from the File menu. The 'Open Folder' option is visible (Box ID 19). I will click on it to proceed.

previous action: I was instructed to click on the 'Open Folder' option in the File menu of Visual Studio Code. The current screen now shows a file picker dialog with options such as 'Open Folder', 'Organize', 'New folder', and a list of folders and icons. This confirms that the file picker GUI has appeared as expected. The next step is to navigate to the 'Documents' directory. I can see an icon labeled 'Documents' (Box ID 44) in the file picker. I will click on it to open the 'Documents' directory.

previous action: I was instructed to click on the 'Documents' icon in the file picker. The current screen now shows the contents of the 'Documents' directory, with several folders listed, such as 'Fundamentals', 'Ipilot', 'Overwatch', 'powershell', 'storage', etc. This confirms that the previous action succeeded. The next step is to select the 'CalculatorApp' folder. However, I do not see a folder named 'CalculatorApp' in the visible list. I will scroll down to check if the 'CalculatorApp' folder is further down in the directory listing.
